_comment: Baked into the NSIS stub via the File directive in installer.nsh and used when the live manifest fetch from updates.shortcutstudio.app fails (offline install, AWS outage, DNS issue). The build-time fallback ships whatever was current when the stub was built; users can always re-run the installer later to pick up a newer manifest.

stub._note: Stub fields are intentionally empty in the baked-in fallback. The in-app updater only references stub.url when the live manifest fetch succeeds and reports a newer version; in that case stub.url comes from the live manifest, not this file.

app._note: App fields are intentionally empty in the baked-in fallback. The fallback's job is only to enumerate components that the stub should fetch — the .7z payload is delivered by electron-builder's own nsis-web mechanism (it's bundled next to the stub, not fetched via this manifest's app.url).

requiredComponents.description: Peer-to-peer transport for shared file processing.
Reverse proxy for the ExecEngine HTTP / FastAPI consumer-peer layer.

optionalComponents.description: Local LLM runtime. Recommended for offline scan.
Local LLM server with a model marketplace UI.
